dependencies.rationale: The breach of contract claim requires the existence of the MSA as the contractual foundation.
Defective design/build/implementation is a premise of the breach of contract allegation.
Delay in delivery is a premise of the breach of contract allegation.
Defective design/build/implementation is a premise of the negligence allegation.
The misrepresentation claim is premised on the oral representation made by Mr Frost.
Misrepresentation requires that the claimant relied on the representation in entering the MSA.
Misrepresentation requires that the representation was false.
Reliance on the representation presupposes the representation was made.
Falsity of the representation presupposes the representation was made.
A breach for late delivery requires the go-live date to have been a fixed contractual obligation.
The materiality of late delivery as a breach is reinforced by time being of the essence.
The breach of the MSA in delivering late requires the MSA and its obligations to be established.
The quantification of delay (seven weeks) is a particularisation of the late delivery breach.
The cause of delay (inadequate resources) is a premise of the delay allegation.
Till failures are caused by the platform's unavailability.
Inability to process sales is caused by the platform's unavailability.
Incorrect stock figures result from the defects in the stock-synchronisation module.
The platform not being of satisfactory quality is premised on the existence of the pleaded defects.
The platform not being fit for purpose is premised on the existence of the pleaded defects.
Wasted expenditure as a head of loss is premised on breach of contract being established.
Loss of profit is premised on breach of contract being established.
Loss of profit during November and December 2024 is causally linked to the platform's unavailability during that period.
Loss of profit is causally linked to the till failures and inability to process sales flowing from unavailability.
Loss of profit is causally linked to the inability to process sales.
Interest is ancillary to and depends on the primary monetary losses being established.
Interest is ancillary to and depends on the primary monetary losses being established.
The total amount claimed aggregates the pleaded heads of loss.
The total amount claimed aggregates the pleaded heads of loss.
The denial of the claimant's no-change assertion is premised on Change Order No. 3 existing.
The denial depends on the claimant having signed Change Order No. 3.
The change of go-live date via Change Order No. 3 requires that the Change Order was signed.
The change of go-live date is explained by the addition of the loyalty module under Change Order No. 3.
The Change Order's legitimacy as a cause of delay depends on it having been requested by the claimant.
The assertion that the go-live date was a target is supported by and connected to the Change Order varying it.
The claimant's acceptance of risk is premised on it having rejected TechFlow's recommendation to defer.
The claimant's acceptance of risk is premised on its own instruction to proceed.
The claimant's rejection of the deferral recommendation presupposes TechFlow made that recommendation.
The instruction to proceed presupposes the claimant had rejected the recommendation to defer.
The denial that the outage was caused by the platform is premised on the assertion it was caused by Northgate Telecom.
The overstatement of loss of profit is supported by the intervening cause of the distribution centre flooding.
The overstatement of loss of profit is supported by the intervening cause of the retail sector downturn.
The quantum of withheld payments depends on the allegation that the claimant wrongfully withheld milestone payments.
The wrongful withholding of payments is supported by the claimant having accepted Phase 1 via the Acceptance Certificate.
